about: A digital marketing company that helps founders and small businesses grow, combining hands-on AI consulting with practical AI software products. We make modern, AI-powered marketing approachable for lean teams.
voice: Friendly, warm, and encouraging — like a knowledgeable friend who happens to run a marketing agency. Approachable and jargon-free: explain AI and marketing ideas in plain language a busy founder can act on. Practical over theoretical, optimistic without hype. Speak to small-business owners and founders directly ('you', 'your team'). Credible but never stuffy.
guidelines: Captions are 1-3 short paragraphs. Open with a relatable hook or question, not a greeting. Keep it skimmable — short sentences, line breaks. 1-3 emoji max, used naturally. Always tie back to a practical takeaway a founder/SMB could use. Never invent statistics, client names, prices, results, or product claims; keep examples illustrative and generic. End with a light, low-pressure invitation to engage (a question or 'save this for later') rather than a hard sell. Hashtags: 6-12, relevant, lowercase, no spaces, mixing marketing + AI + small-business terms.
topics: A simple way a small team can use AI to save hours on their marketing each week
How founders on a tight budget can punch above their weight with AI-assisted marketing
Demystifying an AI marketing concept (in plain English) for non-technical founders
A practical marketing automation an SMB could set up this week
A common AI or marketing mistake small businesses make — and an easy fix

Behind Harvona: how we blend digital marketing with AI to help small businesses grow
One overlooked, free or low-cost way to get more from your existing marketing
How to start with AI in your business without it feeling overwhelming
A myth about AI marketing that's quietly costing small businesses time or money
What good marketing results actually look like for a small business, and how to measure them
How to write marketing copy faster with AI without it sounding robotic
Turning one good idea into a week's worth of social posts
A founder's guide to showing up online consistently when you're short on time
How small businesses can personalize the customer experience using AI
The first three marketing tasks worth automating in a small business
Should you start running paid ads yet? A simple gut-check for founders
How to use AI to understand your customers and what they actually want
What we wish every founder knew before spending money on marketing
Turning the questions customers keep asking into content that attracts more of them
Signs it's time to get help with your marketing instead of doing it all yourself
How AI is changing marketing for local and small businesses this year
A simple weekly marketing routine a busy founder can actually stick to
Free vs. paid AI marketing tools: what's worth it for a lean team
How to sound like a bigger brand without a bigger budget
imageStyle: Warm, modern, editorial-style image showing REAL, relatable people — a small-business owner or a lean team of 2-3 — actively working on digital marketing together and interacting with each other (e.g., collaborating over a laptop while reviewing a simple analytics dashboard, brainstorming social-content ideas at a whiteboard, or filming short-form content with a phone on a small tripod). Show genuine, expressive human faces and natural body language and eye contact — NOT abstract, faceless, or blob-like figures. Bright, optimistic, natural lighting; a cohesive warm palette (soft amber, peach, gentle blue). Professional yet human and approachable, never sterile corporate stock. Any on-screen graphics (charts, feeds, dashboards) must be simple and abstract with NO readable text, words, letters, numbers, or logos rendered anywhere in the image.
